text:
it wasn't about the caffeine — neither in itself *nor* for gut motility (which I've helped w/ small diet changes). 

the main thing, i came (w/ help) to realize, was the way the sugary-beverage ritual was both a cornerstone of my reward system *and* my state-change system.

raw.full_text:
it wasn't about the caffeine — neither in itself *nor* for gut motility (which I've helped w/ small diet changes). 

the main thing, i came (w/ help) to realize, was the way the sugary-beverage ritual was both a cornerstone of my reward system *and* my state-change system.

text:
the caffeine has an element of "prep w stimmies," but the real meat of the sugary-beverage ritual  is 

"serve as a reward for sitting down to work, and help me get into work mode" 

and 

"serve as reward for a day's work, help me get into goblin-mode."

text:
homemade chai lattes are just objectively better at this.

going into work mode, there's still enough sugar and sensation to generate some pep, but not too much caffeine to get anxious.

and since goblin-mode is a late-night thing, less caffeine is obviously better. (tea optn'l!)

text: it's still a lot of sugar the way i make it. but it's not ice-cold, and it's not high-fructose corn syrup and carbonic acid. it's a *lot* more hydrating and gentler on my bladder (a *major* issue this whole time). it's also a lot cheaper for making at home than a soda-stream.

text:
*plus*, making it good had downstream effects in the kitchen: 

1) having oat milk on hand is great for more than just lattes, but i didn't use it often enough to keep some around.  now i do

2) own a mortal and pestle now, with cinnamon sticks and cloves. spice tech tree unlock.

text:
but the real kicker: my homemade chai latte has rapidly become the best drink I can get my hands on. 

dr. pepper just isn't as good, even in ideal fountain conditions. and what i make, i like better than any chai latte i can get elsewhere. it outcompetes everything in its niche!

text:
knowing that dr. pepper isn't as good health-wise is way less tangible than knowing that a chai latte *tastes better* and is *easier* and *cheaper* and *more fulfilling*. 

there have been no tradeoffs, it has all just been gain precipitated by patient, honest self-acceptance.
